items.headline: Stocks Decline Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. stocks opened lower today, continuing a trend of declines, as concerns over rising oil prices and Treasury yields weighed on market sentiment. The S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq all registered losses at the opening bell. Geopolitical developments surrounding Iran, including a directive from its Supreme Leader regarding enriched uranium, contributed to oil price volatility and heightened inflation concerns. Adding to the cautious outlook, U.S. business activity, while expanding in May, saw input costs rise at their steepest rate since late 2022, leading to lower sales and increased job losses in the services sector. Walmart's latest guidance further signaled a shift in consumer spending, with higher-income shoppers reportedly trading down to discount retailers.

items.statContext: Down 0.32% today, after declining 0.45% at the opening bell.

items.headline: Gates Industrial surges on acquisition optimism
items.summary: Power transmission belt maker Gates Industrial surged 15.8% in premarket trading amidst optimism surrounding its acquisition of The Timken Company's belts business.

items.headline: Applied Digital soars on AI campus lease agreement
items.summary: Shares of Applied Digital gained 9% after the company announced a 15-year take-or-pay lease agreement for its Polaris Forge 3 AI campus, valued at an estimated $7.5 billion in base contracted revenue.

items.headline: e.l.f. Beauty climbs on strong Q4 results
items.summary: e.l.f. Beauty shares rose 9% following the release of stronger-than-expected FQ4 results, driven by robust growth from its Rhode and Naturium brands.

items.headline: Intuit tumbles on workforce cuts despite strong earnings
items.summary: Intuit Inc. tumbled nearly 14% in premarket trading after the TurboTax and QuickBooks parent company announced plans to cut approximately 17% of its workforce, overshadowing better-than-expected earnings.

items.headline: Walmart shares fall on weaker full-year outlook
items.summary: Walmart shares declined nearly 2% after the retail giant issued a weaker-than-expected full-year outlook alongside its fiscal first-quarter results, citing higher fuel costs.

items.headline: ChargePoint Holdings declines
items.summary: ChargePoint Holdings Inc. lost 4.2% in premarket trading.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Edge Lower, Business Activity Expands
items.summary: U.S. applications for unemployment benefits fell by 3,000 to 209,000 in the week ending May 16, coming in below economist expectations. Meanwhile, the S&P Global Flash U.S. Composite PMI remained unchanged at 51.7 in May, indicating continued expansion in private sector business activity, though manufacturing showed improvement while services lagged. However, firms reported the steepest rise in input costs since late 2022, contributing to increased selling prices and job losses.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports: Mixed Signals from Retail and Tech
items.summary: Several major companies reported earnings today. Walmart ($WMT) disappointed investors with a weaker-than-expected full-year outlook, leading to a share decline. Intuit ($INTU) shares tumbled despite better-than-expected earnings, as the company announced significant workforce reductions. In contrast, Eli Lilly ($LLY) saw its shares rise following positive late-stage trial results for its next-generation obesity drug. Deere & Company ($DE) reported second-quarter net income of $1.773 billion, maintaining its fiscal 2026 outlook. NVIDIA ($NVDA), having reported strong quarterly results and an optimistic forecast late Wednesday, saw a muted stock reaction as investors potentially locked in profits. NIO Inc. ($NIO) announced a significant 112.2% increase in total revenues for Q1 2026 compared to the previous year.

items.headline: Government Boosts Quantum Computing, SpaceX Eyes IPO
items.summary: The Trump administration is reportedly awarding $2 billion in grants to nine quantum computing firms, including government equity stakes, to boost the nascent industry. Separately, Elon Musk's SpaceX is targeting a Starship launch today, following reports of its IPO filing.
